text:
The edition-specific import path handling spans three layers:

1. **Prelude injection** (`compiler/rustc_builtin_macros/src/standard_library_imports.rs`): The `inject()` function selects the edition-specific prelude module (rust_2015/rust_2018/rust_2021/rust_2024) based on `sess.psess.edition`, generates `extern crate` with `#[macro_use]`, and builds the import path with edition-dependent `PathRoot` behavior (present only for 2015).

2. **Library prelude definitions**: `library/std/src/prelude/mod.rs` and `library/core/src/prelude/mod.rs` define edition-specific prelude modules (rust_2015, rust_2018 re-export v1; rust_2021 adds TryFrom/TryInto/FromIterator; rust_2024 adds Future/IntoFuture). The v1 modules (`library/std/src/prelude/v1.rs`, `library/core/src/prelude/v1.rs`) contain the base prelude exports.

3. **Resolver edition behavior**: `compiler/rustc_resolve/src/ident.rs` implements the scope visitor with edition-aware logic—line 160 shows `MacroUsePrelude` is visited when `use_prelude || rust_2015` (meaning 2015 always sees macro_use prelude, while 2018+ only sees it in modules with implicit prelude). Lines 1787-1798 handle `::path` resolution differently for 2015 vs 2018 editions. `compiler/rustc_resolve/src/build_reduced_graph.rs` handles edition-specific crate-relative vs module-relative import resolution (lines 545-562, 363-376) and `process_macro_use_imports`. `compiler/rustc_resolve/src/lib.rs` defines the `Scope` enum with `MacroUsePrelude`, `StdLibPrelude`, `ExternPrelude` variants. `compiler/rustc_resolve/src/check_unused.rs` handles unused extern crate linting with edition awareness. `compiler/rustc_resolve/src/macros.rs` passes edition to macro compilation. `compiler/rustc_hir_typeck/src/method/prelude_edition_lints.rs` implements lints for prelude collision detection (RUST_2021_PRELUDE_COLLISIONS, RUST_2024_PRELUDE_COLLISIONS).
